attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a rinsed bar of soap in a drawer.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a bathroom environment with a focus on a vanity area. The relevant objects and their properties are as follows:

1.  **Objects and their types**:
    -   **Receptacles**: There is one green cabinet with multiple drawers. Each drawer is a receptacle and is openable. The cabinet is positioned against the wall on the left side of the scene. The top surface of the cabinet serves as a countertop.
    -   **Objects**: There are two pink rectangular bars of soap on the countertop. One is positioned near the left edge, and the other is further to the right. There is also a yellow bottle (likely soap or lotion) and a roll of toilet paper on the countertop. A dark brown, circular sink with a silver faucet is mounted on the countertop. A knife is not visibly present in the scene. The sink is a receptacle.

2.  **Quantities**:
    -   Receptacles: 1 cabinet (with multiple drawers, treated as one unit for placement), 1 sink.
    -   Objects: 2 bars of soap, 1 bottle, 1 roll of toilet paper.

3.  **Spatial relationships**:
    -   The green cabinet is against the left wall, extending horizontally. The countertop is on top of the cabinet.
    -   The two bars of soap are on the countertop. One is near the left edge (closer to the toilet paper), and the other is near the right edge (closer to the yellow bottle).
    -   The sink is mounted on the countertop, positioned towards the left side, above the cabinet.
    -   The yellow bottle and toilet paper are on the countertop, to the left of the rightmost bar of soap.
    -   The robot is not initially at any object’s location.

4.  **Current state of objects relevant to the task**:
    -   The bars of soap are not currently in any receptacle (not in the sink or drawers).
    -   The bars of soap are not clean (not rinsed).
    -   The drawers of the cabinet are not opened (assumed closed unless specified).
    -   The sink is not currently being used for cleaning (no object is being held in it for rinsing).

5.  **Colors and visual properties**:
    -   The cabinet is green with visible handles on the drawers.
    -   The countertop is light gray.
    -   The bars of soap are pink and rectangular.
    -   The sink is dark brown with a marbled texture and has a silver faucet.
    -   The bottle is yellow and cylindrical.
    -   The toilet paper is white and cylindrical.
    -   The floor is tiled with light gray square tiles, and there is a gray rectangular rug in the center.

The task requires placing a rinsed bar of soap in a drawer. This implies the robot must first pick up a bar of soap, rinse it in the sink, and then place it in one of the drawers of the cabinet. The drawers must be opened before placing the soap inside.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem place_rinsed_soap_in_drawer)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        soap1 soap2 - object
        bottle - object
        toilet_paper - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot soap1))
        (not (atLocation robot soap2))
        (not (atLocation robot bottle))
        (not (atLocation robot toilet_paper))

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable cabinet)
        (openable sink)

        ; Drawers are closed initially
        (not (opened cabinet))

        ; Soap bars are on the countertop (not in any receptacle)
        (not (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet))
        (not (inReceptacle soap2 cabinet))
        (not (inReceptacle soap1 sink))
        (not (inReceptacle soap2 sink))

        ; Soap bars are not clean
        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (isClean soap2))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; One bar of soap is clean and in the cabinet
            (isClean soap1)
            (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem place_rinsed_soap_in_drawer)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        soap1 soap2 - object
        bottle - object
        toilet_paper - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot soap1))
        (not (atLocation robot soap2))
        (not (atLocation robot bottle))
        (not (atLocation robot toilet_paper))

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable cabinet)
        (openable sink)

        ; Drawers are closed initially
        (not (opened cabinet))

        ; Soap bars are on the countertop (not in any receptacle)
        (not (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet))
        (not (inReceptacle soap2 cabinet))
        (not (inReceptacle soap1 sink))
        (not (inReceptacle soap2 sink))

        ; Soap bars are not clean
        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (isClean soap2))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; One bar of soap is clean and in the cabinet
            (isClean soap1)
            (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        )
    )
)

The scene depicts a bathroom environment with several objects relevant to the planning task. The primary objects of interest are:

1.  **Agent**: A robotic agent is present in the scene, though not explicitly visible in the provided images. It is initially not located at any specific object's position.

2.  **Receptacles**:
    *   **Cabinet/Drawer Unit**: A green cabinet with multiple drawers is visible. The top surface is light grey. This unit functions as a receptacle for storing items. Based on the images, it appears to have at least three drawers on the front face, each with a small, dark reddish-brown handle. The cabinet is positioned against a wall and extends from the left side of the frame towards the center. It is openable, and its drawers can be opened and closed. Currently, all drawers are closed.
    *   **Sink**: A round, brown marble-textured sink is present. It has a silver faucet and a visible drain. The sink is mounted on a black hexagonal base. It is positioned to the right of the cabinet. The sink is a receptacle and is used for cleaning objects. It is currently empty and not in use.

3.  **Objects**:
    *   **Bar of Soap**: Two pink, rectangular bars of soap are visible. One is located on the countertop of the green cabinet, near the right edge. The other is also on the countertop, slightly to the left of the first one. These are the target objects for the task.
    *   **Knife**: A yellow knife with a white handle is visible on the countertop, positioned between the two bars of soap. It is a distinct object, not a receptacle.
    *   **Toilet Paper**: A roll of white toilet paper is on the countertop, to the left of the knife.
    *   **Soap Dispenser**: A grey soap dispenser with a brown pump is on the countertop, to the left of the toilet paper.
    *   **Rug**: A large, grey rectangular rug is on the tiled floor, positioned in front of the cabinet and sink area. It is not a receptacle or an object relevant to the domain's actions.

**Spatial Relationships and Current State**:

*   The **agent** is not currently at the location of any object.
*   The **bars of soap** are on the countertop of the **cabinet**. They are not currently in any receptacle, nor are they clean, hot, cool, or sliced.
*   The **knife** is on the countertop of the **cabinet**, next to the soap. It is not being held by the agent.
*   The **cabinet** is a receptacle that is currently closed (not opened).
*   The **sink** is a receptacle and is currently empty and not being used for cleaning.
*   The **toilet paper** and **soap dispenser** are on the countertop of the **cabinet**.

The task requires the agent to pick up one of the bars of soap, clean it in the sink, and then place it inside one of the drawers of the cabinet. The current state of all relevant objects is neutral, with no actions having been performed yet. The agent must first navigate to the location of a bar of soap to begin the task.

```pddl
(define (problem place_rinsed_soap_in_drawer)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        soap1 - object
        soap2 - object
        knife - object
        toilet_paper - object
        soap_dispenser - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toilet_paper))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap_dispenser))
        
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        
        (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        (inReceptacle soap2 cabinet)
        (inReceptacle knife cabinet)
        (inReceptacle toilet_paper cabinet)
        (inReceptacle soap_dispenser cabinet)
        
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (isClean soap2))
        (not (isHot soap1))
        (not (isHot soap2))
        (not (isCool soap1))
        (not (isCool soap2))
        (not (isSliced soap1))
        (not (isSliced soap2))
        (not (isOn soap1))
        (not (isOn soap2))
        (not (isToggled soap1))
        (not (isToggled soap2))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        (isClean soap1)
    ))
)
```

(define (problem place_rinsed_soap_in_drawer)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        soap1 - object
        soap2 - object
        knife - object
        toilet_paper - object
        soap_dispenser - object
    )
    (:init

        (not (atLocation agent1 sink))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toilet_paper))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap_dispenser))
        
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        
        (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        (inReceptacle soap2 cabinet)
        (inReceptacle knife cabinet)
        (inReceptacle toilet_paper cabinet)
        (inReceptacle soap_dispenser cabinet)

        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (isClean soap2))
        (not (isHot soap1))
        (not (isHot soap2))
        (not (isCool soap1))
        (not (isCool soap2))
        (not (isSliced soap1))
        (not (isSliced soap2))
        (not (isOn soap1))
        (not (isOn soap2))
        (not (isToggled soap1))
        (not (isToggled soap2))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap1 cabinet)
        (isClean soap1)
    ))
)

You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object

    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)

 )


;; agent toggle object

    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)

            )
 )
